title: MachineFromLibraryInput Object
description: Object used as input to create a machine from library URL.  Used by "Machine.create(MachineInput)" method.  The object holds the data needed to create a machine from the specified library URL

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: create
methods.description: Creates a MachineFromLibraryInput object to be used as input for Machine.create method, in order to create a machine from a library location.

properties.name: ignoreSimulationModel
properties.description: Gets and sets whether or not to ignore the simulation model when creating or loading the machine. If set to true the simulation model will not be set on the new machine.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: url
properties.description: The URL path to the library machine.

classType.title: MachineFromLibraryInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

create.title: MachineFromLibraryInput.create Method
create.description: Creates a MachineFromLibraryInput object to be used as input for Machine.create method, in order to create a machine from a library location.
create.return_description: Returns newly created MachineFromLibraryInput object in a valid state.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL path to the library machine.

ignoreSimulationModel.title: MachineFromLibraryInput.ignoreSimulationModel Property
ignoreSimulationModel.description: Gets and sets whether or not to ignore the simulation model when creating or loading the machine. If set to true the simulation model will not be set on the new machine. Remarks

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineFromLibraryInput_var.ignoreSimulationModel

# Set the value of the property.
machineFromLibraryInput_var.ignoreSimulationModel = propertyValue

isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineFromLibraryInput_var.isValid

objectType.title: MachineFromLibraryInput.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineFromLibraryInput_var.objectType

url.title: MachineFromLibraryInput.url Property
url.description: The URL path to the library machine.

syntax.python:
